goal: Assist user in adjusting to a new permanent 8:00 on-site start every weekday.
Preserve a 20–30 minute workout and a relaxed breakfast despite the 8:00 start.
Adopt a mixed workout timing plan (2–3 fuller morning sessions on remote/partner days, 15–20 minute morning sessions on office days, and 1–2 longer evening sessions) to support energy and sleep.
Minimize burnout by implementing low-effort contingency plans during chaotic weeks.
constraints: Permanent 8:00 on-site start; out-the-door by 7:30; weekday-only schedule.
Daycare opens at 8:30; partner handles drop-off.
Incorporate a commute buffer: baseline 15-minute buffer; option for 20–25-minute buffer for higher certainty.
decisions: Create a 7-day sleep/wake plan with daily wake times and bedtimes.
Create a weekly meal-prep plan for breakfasts, lunches, and daycare snacks.
Map the mixed workout timing plan to your exact work/remote days and daycare schedule.
Decide on a trial framework for occasional remote work (frequency, scope, review cadence).
Select 2–4 contingency options to automate this first week.
Decide on a carpool vs solo trial framework (criteria, duration, review cadence).
Choose 1–2 changes to try for a week to address mid-afternoon slump (e.g., earlier lunch, planned protein snack, 5-minute walk).
entities: 7-day sleep/wake plan with daily wake times (Day 1: 7:15, Day 2: 7:00, Day 3: 6:45, Day 4: 6:30, Day 5: 6:15, Day 6: 6:15, Day 7: 6:15).

Meal-prep framework for weekday mornings: batch-cook 1–2x/week; overnight oats, smoothie packs, egg muffins; pack the night before; freezer-friendly options for breakfasts, lunches, and daycare snacks.
Commute buffer guideline: baseline 15-minute buffer; 20–25 minutes for higher certainty scenarios.
Mixed weekly workout timing plan: morning sessions 2–3 fuller sessions on remote/partner days, 15–20 minute mornings on office days, and 1–2 longer evening sessions, to support energy and sleep.

Hour-by-hour sample weekday outline with exact times: 6:15 Wake; 6:20–6:40 Workout (20 min); 6:40–6:50 Shower and dress; 6:50–7:05 Breakfast; 7:05–7:10 Final pack; 7:10–7:15 Hand-off to partner; 7:15 Leave home; 7:15–7:45 Commute; ~7:45–7:55 Arrival/settle.

Weekend prep checklist (new): separate items for clothes, lunches, daycare bag, work bag & commute items, household & logistics, Sunday evening run-through, labeling & safety, and a small habit (3–5 minute visual check).

Remote flexibility request plan: one remote day per week or ad-hoc remote when daycare is unavailable, with a 6-week review.
Carpool option with nearby coworker: criteria and backup plan.
Mid-day slump fixes: hydration, short movement (5–10 min), light exposure, quick protein snack (Greek yogurt, egg, nuts), breathing reset.
Nutrition timing: shift lunch to ~4–5 hours after waking; include a protein + fiber + healthy fat at lunch; plan a mid-afternoon snack (protein + fat or fiber).
Caffeine strategy: consume a small cup ~20–30 minutes before the slump; avoid caffeine late in the day.
Schedule tweaks: front-load demanding work in mid-morning; reserve mid-afternoon for routine tasks or short meetings; schedule a pre-slump walk if possible.
Sample routine: Wake ~6:15; lunch ~11:30–12:00 with protein + veg + whole grain; snack ~14:00–14:30 (protein + fat/fiber).
Pick 1–2 changes for a week: earlier lunch + planned protein snack + 5–10 min walk.
open: Clarify remote days flexibility and partner drop-off time adjustments.
Update the 7-day wake/sleep plan to reflect adjusted departure buffer (e.g., shift wake times and bedtimes as needed).
Develop an early-meetings alertness plan (0–60 minute pre-meeting routine, light exposure, hydration, movement, caffeine guidance, and a micro-checklist).
Validate feasibility of the mixed workout plan with sleep/energy tracking and adjust accordingly.
Create a concise plan to raise occasional remote flexibility, including a sample script or talking points, a trial period, and review criteria.
Decide on and implement 2–4 contingency options in the first week with automation and review criteria.
Carpool decision: evaluate a nearby coworker carpool option, define decision criteria, confirmation timing, and backup plan.
Schedule and run a weekend practice morning with partner to baseline timings and friction points; record results; test baseline and worst-case variation.
